mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index captures expanding manufacturing and utilities output, which boosts aggregate corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO acceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the strategy’s information coefficient falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater increase in the unemployment rate over a rolling three-month window triggers a deterioration in household income expectations and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences, or the rank correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns is greater than -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal overheated final demand that raises input cost pressures and tightens monetary policy expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns (positive hit rate > 55%) or the interaction term exhibits a rank IC above zero over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points accelerates discount rate expansion and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% and rises by at least 25bp in a month, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45 percent of instances, or the rank IC of FEDFUNDS changes is greater than -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment signal rising household income and durable consumption demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the rank IC of PAYEMS changes is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month annualized inflation rate exceeding six percent as measured by CPIAUCSL forces a forward repricing of real discount rates and erodes corporate margin expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change above 6% are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the rank correlation between inflation changes and forward returns is above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below four percent and CPIAUCSL posts a month-over-month increase above zero point five percent, the combination of tight labor markets and accelerating inflation generates expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve intervention, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with rising inflation
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE below 4% and month-over-month CPIAUCSL above 0.5% fails to produce negative 60-day returns (positive hit rate > 50%) or the interaction term rank IC is greater than zero over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts indicate weakening residential investment and deteriorating forward-looking demand for durable goods and construction materials, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the rank IC of HOUST momentum is above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
